changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "From The Scrapbook IX"

TextENGNew: It was the Sonozaki family that dramatically expanded its influence after the war.
The head of the family at that time was Oryou Sonozaki, who still holds that position today.
Oryou Sonozaki has grown old, and today one can barely glimpse the formidable figure she once was.
However, from what I have been told, she was acclaimed as the finest leader in the family's entire history.
Now elderly, aside from going out several times a week for lessons, she spends her days quietly at home, and appears to delegate most ceremonial duties to her heir, Mion.
(There are various theories as to why the succession skipped her daughter in favor of her granddaughter Mion, but it is rumored to be connected to a falling-out with her daughter and son-in-law.)
changeReason: Old translation uses 'disownment' while new uses 'falling-out.' The Japanese 勘当騒ぎ specifically refers to a disownment incident, so the change from the formal act of disownment to a general disagreement alters the understood severity of the family conflict.

TextENGNew: That freewheeling girl, Mion Sonozaki, still shows none of the gravitas expected of a future head of the family.

However, as one who carries the Sonozaki bloodline and has been granted the right to bear the name of the demon,@kshe is surely a formidable individual who skillfully conceals her fangs and claws -- much like the leaders who came before her.
